topStories.title: Quote of the day by Michael Price: "Wall Street tends to overreact when it gets worried about earnings"

This pattern sees short-term fears trigger sharp selloffs, even when businesses remain fundamentally strong. Such market overreactions can present opportunities for patient investors. Focusing on long-term fundamentals helps navigate these periods of volatility. Corporate performance and economic reality ultimately prevail over short-term panic.
